body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The current escaping logic only handles single quotes for the shell command but fails to account for double quotes within the AppleScript string literal. If the `pkg_path` contains a double quote (e.g., in an application folder name), the AppleScript `do shell script "..."` will be terminated prematurely, causing a syntax error. 

A more robust approach is to use AppleScript's `quoted form of` property, which handles all necessary shell escaping automatically, while ensuring double quotes in the path are escaped for the AppleScript string itself.

```suggestion
                r#"do shell script "installer -pkg " & quoted form of "{}" & " -target /" with administrator privileges"#,
                pkg_path.to_string_lossy().replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("\"", "\\\"")
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The variable `method` is assigned the result of `install_blackhole_from_bundle`, which returns `Result<()>`. Consequently, `method` is of type `()` (unit), making the log message on line 186 uninformative. You should rename this variable or remove the assignment if the specific installation method isn't being returned by this function.

```suggestion
    install_blackhole_from_bundle(&app_handle).map_err(|e| {
```
